info.title: Supplier Risk Engagements API
info.description: The Supplier Risk Engagements API retrieves engagement data including engagement projects, issues, and modular questionnaires.

tags.name: Supplier Risk Engagements API
tags.description: Exports the engagement data from your engagement projects, issues, and modular questionnaires.

get.description: This API will fetch paginated details of engagements based on the limit  configured in the system. Optionally you can pass in additional filter  parameters to get only a subset of engagements. API accepts query param  $skip to get the next page response.

parameters.name: realm
parameters.description: This parameter specifies one of the realm names for which the client application is authenticated and provides access to the data in that realm. Try it out on Sandbox with: s4All-3

parameters.name: $count
parameters.description:
When value is true, total count of records will be returned.

Usage explained as below:

$count=true: returns total count of records after applying the filters.

$count=false or no $count is passed: page meta data will not be returned in the response.

parameters.name: $top
parameters.description:
Value passed in this query parameter decides the page limit or page size of the current page returned.

Usage explained as below:

$top=10: returns first 10 records for that page from the offset ID and value has to be greater than 0 and lesser than or equal to system defined page limit. Here system defined page limit is 100.

parameters.name: $skip
parameters.description:
offsetId/pageToken for pagination.

No offsetId/pageToken is required when the API is invoked for the first time. Response header will contain X-Pagination-Token and response body will contain pageToken if the records are not exhausted. The pageToken returned for a page can be passed to the $skip token to get the next page.

Usage explained as below:

scenario 1 - $skip=0 or not passing any query parameter are both same. API will return page 1 records from start depending on the $top query parameter value if present else the configured page limit(100)

scenario 2 - $skip=10 - API skips first 10 records and retrieves the successive page records depending on the $top query parameter value if present else the configured page limit(100).

parameters.name: $filter
parameters.description:
This request parameter takes updatedDateFrom and updatedDateTo parameters in yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ssZ GMT time zone format and returns all records in the range depending on the configured page limit.

Usage explained as below:

scenario 1 - $filter=updatedDateFrom ge 2018-09-29T12:20:51Z and updatedDateTo le 2019-10-04T12:20:51Z - retrieves all records that fall between this range depending on the configured page limit

scenario 2 - $filter=updatedDateTo le 2019-10-04T12:20:51Z - retrieves all records from start till the date specified above depending on the configured page limit

scenario 3 - $filter=updatedDateFrom ge 2018-09-29T12:20:51Z - API throws InvalidParameter Exception as updatedDateTo is a mandatory parameter.

get.summary: Retrieve engagement project details by engagement workspace ID
get.description: This API will fetch all engagement project details for a given project ID.

parameters.name: realm
parameters.description: Identify the requested realm.

parameters.name: workspaceId
parameters.description: Identify the Vendor ID.

example: The API key passed in the request is not valid.
The API key is missing in the header.
The API key provided in the header does not match with the API key in the token.
Invalid or missing request parameters: sm_vendor_id or realm.
Realm YYY in the request does not match with realms in the token.
Realm YYY in the request is not available.
Check the realm in the request. Realm value should not be null or empty.

InvalidInputParameter.description: If your URL is not constructed properly, you have trailing spaces in URl, request body format is wrong etc.

RateLimitError.description: You have exceeded the maximum permitted query rate. Refer to the developer  portal discovery page for this API for the applicable query rates.

InternalServerError.description: The risk data could not be retrieved for the supplier. This may be due  to connection issues or the server is down. Try again after a while.

GatewayTimeoutError.description: The server timeout time has been exceeded, possibly due to network traffic  or reliability issues. Retry your query.
